agent.name: Patient Awareness Agent
agent.description: Manages paid advertising (Google Ads, Meta Ads) and organic discovery to identify and capture high-intent chronic pain patients. Optimizes ad spend for lowest cost per lead while maintaining lead quality.

responsibilities: Manage Google Ads campaigns for chronic pain search keywords
Manage Meta Ads (Facebook/Instagram) for awareness and retargeting
Monitor organic lead sources (SEO, referral, direct)
Capture leads from landing pages and form submissions
Score lead quality based on pain level, intent, and source
Optimize ad spend and bids based on conversion signals
A/B test ad creative, landing pages, and messaging
Tag and segment leads for downstream nurture agents
Monitor ROAS and cost per lead (CPL) against targets
Escalate creative/messaging issues to growth team

stages.name: Ingest
stages.description: Collect advertising and behavioral data from all sources
steps: Listen for PostHog events: page_view, landing_page_submission, form_submission
Consume Google Ads API: conversions, clicks, impressions, cost
Consume Meta Marketing API: conversions, impressions, spend
Consume landing page analytics: traffic source, referrer, device type
Match user IDs across platforms (Google Analytics ID, Meta pixel, email)

stages.name: Reason
stages.description: Analyze data to determine lead quality and next actions
steps: Calculate lead quality score (0-100) based on: pain type (keyword intent), location match, device type, time spent on page
Determine traffic source quality (paid vs. organic)
Assess ad performance: CTR, conversion rate, ROAS
Identify underperforming ad sets or keywords
Segment leads by quality tier: high (>75), medium (50-75), low (<50)
Determine if CPL is trending above/below target

decision_logic.if_lead_quality_high: Fast-track to nurture sequence, assign highest email priority
decision_logic.if_lead_quality_low: Retargeting ads, nurture with lower email frequency
decision_logic.if_roas_below_target: Reduce spend on underperforming keywords/ads, reallocate to high performers
decision_logic.if_cpl_above_target: A/B test new creative or landing page variant
decision_logic.if_organic_lead_high_quality: Scale organic investments (SEO, content), tag source

stages.name: Execute
stages.description: Perform advertising and lead management actions
steps: Adjust Google Ads bid multipliers based on quality signals (device, location, time of day)
Create/update Meta lookalike audiences from high-quality lead cohorts
Add qualified lead to Loops with source tag (google_ads, meta_ads, organic)
Set lead quality score and pain type in Loops
Trigger landing page variant based on traffic source
Queue first nurture email immediately (triggered by Loops automation)
Log lead capture event to PostHog with all metadata
Update ad account performance metrics to dashboard

stages.name: Log
stages.description: Record all actions and metrics for tracking and optimization
steps: Log lead_captured event: email, source, quality_score, pain_type, location
Track cost_per_lead (cumulative by source)
Track lead_quality_score distribution
Track conversion_rate by source (landing page → email captured)
Track ROAS by campaign
Track A/B test results (control vs. variant CTR, conversion rate)
Append all logs to agent execution history (PostHog custom events)

stages.name: Escalate
stages.description: Route issues and high-value decisions to humans
steps: If ROAS < 1.5 for 3 consecutive days → Flag for growth team review (creative needs refresh)
If CPL > $15 for 3 consecutive days → Flag for bid/targeting adjustment
If landing page conversion rate drops >15% → Escalate to product/marketing team
If >10% of leads bounce immediately (high bounce rate) → Review landing page quality
If unusual traffic pattern detected (10x normal volume) → Alert fraud detection team
If paid source quality drops suddenly → Review ad account for policy violations

triggers.name: hourly_metrics_review
triggers.description: Review advertising metrics hourly for quality and spend efficiency
triggers.action: Execute Ingest → Reason → Execute → Log → Escalate cycle

triggers.name: daily_budget_check
triggers.description: Ensure daily ad spend is within budget and hitting targets
triggers.action: Review total spend, ROAS, CPL; adjust bids if needed

triggers.name: weekly_ab_test_launch
triggers.description: Launch new creative or landing page A/B tests
triggers.condition: Growth team has new variants ready
triggers.action: Set up A/B test in ad platform, allocate 10% traffic to variant

triggers.name: ad_policy_violation
triggers.description: Monitor for ad policy violations (banned keywords, creative issues)
triggers.action: Alert growth team, pause offending ads, request review

integrations.fallback: If API down, use manual Google Ads UI; escalate to growth team
If API down, use manual Ads Manager; escalate to growth team
If API fails, queue events in message buffer; retry hourly until success
If API down, queue events locally; retry when available

functional: All leads captured with complete data (email, source, pain type)
Lead quality scoring consistent and accurate (validated against manual review)
CTR and conversion rates match ad platform reporting (within 1%)
ROAS calculated correctly and matches historical performance
performance: Agent response time < 5 seconds (from event to action)
reliability: All triggers fire within 1 minute of event

error_handling.policy: Fail safely, escalate issues, retry transient failures

error_cases.handling: Log error, alert engineering team, pause campaign changes (read-only mode)
error_cases.escalation: If >30 min unresolved, escalate to CTO

error_cases.handling: Backoff immediately, queue requests in buffer
error_cases.escalation: If consistent rate limiting, request quota increase

error_cases.handling: Reject submission, return error to user, log for monitoring
error_cases.escalation: If >5% rejection rate, flag to product team

error_cases.handling: Default to medium quality (score=50), capture as much data as available
error_cases.retry: Re-score if missing data arrives later

error_cases.handling: Pause affected ad immediately, log violation details
error_cases.escalation: Alert growth team immediately for manual review + fix

error_cases.handling: Log event, alert monitoring system, may be fraud
error_cases.escalation: Alert security team, may pause campaign temporarily

startup_checklist: Test connection to all data sources
Perform dry-run of first workflow cycle
